text:
In the quiet town of Pinehaven, tucked between its pine-covered hills and serene lakes, lived a wise old owl named Oliver. He was known for his deep voice that could soothe the restless soul of any young hare who dared to stray into his territory.

One stormy autumn evening, a young rabbit named Hazel found herself disheartened by the dreary weather. The leaves were a vivid crimson, the sky an ominous gray, and the wind howled as if it wanted to frighten her away. Hazel shuffled back and forth in front of Oliver’s nest, hoping for a sign of wisdom.

“You look unhappy,” Oliver hooted gently. “What troubles you, dear?”

Hazel sniffled. “Just this dreary day, Oliver. Everything seems so gloomy.”

“Ah,” Oliver said thoughtfully. “But even a broomstick can become a masterpiece if held right.” He peered into Hazel’s sad eyes. “Life has its seasons, my young friend.
